item.title: Python as a scientific programming language.
item.description:
Python has become one of the most widely used programming languages in the scientific community. Its versatility and ease of use have made it a go-to language for a wide range of scientific applications, from data analysis to simulations and modeling.

One of the key reasons why Python has gained so much popularity in scientific programming is its large ecosystem of scientific libraries. These libraries provide a wealth of tools for data analysis, visualization, and scientific computing, making it easy for scientists to perform complex analyses and simulations quickly and efficiently.

One of the most popular scientific libraries in Python is NumPy. This library provides support for large, multi-dimensional arrays and matrices, along with a range of mathematical functions for performing operations on these arrays. Another popular library is Pandas, which provides support for data manipulation, analysis, and visualization. With these tools, scientists can easily work with large datasets and perform complex analyses, all within the Python environment.

Python’s readability and ease of use also make it a great choice for scientists who are not necessarily expert programmers. Its syntax is simple and easy to learn, making it accessible to researchers from a variety of scientific fields. This allows scientists to focus on their research rather than struggling with complex programming languages.

In addition, Python’s open-source nature makes it easy for scientists to collaborate and share code with one another. Scientists can share their code on platforms like GitHub, making it easy for others to reproduce their results and build on their research.

Python is also versatile enough to be used in a wide range of scientific applications. It can be used for data analysis, simulations, modeling, and even machine learning. Its flexibility makes it a valuable tool for scientists in a variety of fields, including biology, physics, and engineering.

In conclusion, Python has become a popular choice for scientific programming due to its large ecosystem of scientific libraries, ease of use, readability, versatility, and open-source nature. With its wide range of applications and powerful tools, Python is helping scientists to perform complex analyses and simulations quickly and efficiently, ultimately advancing scientific research and discovery.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: Park Gorky
item.description:
After finishing my classes at the University of RUDN, I felt the urge to explore a bit more of Moscow’s beauty. So, I decided to take the metro to Gorky Park and enjoy a walk through this iconic landmark.

As I exited the metro station, the first thing that caught my attention was the stunning lighting in the park. It was like the whole place was covered with a warm blanket of light, creating a dreamy atmosphere that felt magical. I couldn’t wait to begin my exploration.

I started my walk by following the wide, paved path that led deeper into the park. The first thing that I saw was a large pond with a stunning fountain in the center. The water sparkled under the golden lights, and I could hear the sound of the water trickling from the fountain. The view was so soothing that I found myself taking deep breaths of the fresh, cool air.

As I continued my walk, I found myself surrounded by towering trees and lush greenery. The path wound its way through the park, taking me past tranquil ponds, ornate fountains, and carefully tended gardens. The sound of my footsteps on the path and the rustling of leaves underfoot was the only sound that broke the stillness of the night. It was like the whole park belonged to me.

As I walked, I came across a group of people gathered around a small stage. A band was playing lively music, and the crowd was swaying and dancing along. The energy was infectious, and I found myself smiling and tapping my foot along with the beat. I stayed for a while, enjoying the music and the atmosphere before continuing my walk.

As the night wore on, the park began to empty out, and the once-bustling paths grew quiet. The only sound was the distant hum of traffic and the occasional rustle of leaves in the wind. I took my time walking back to the metro station, savoring every moment of this peaceful and beautiful place.

As I left the park and headed back to the metro station, I couldn’t help but feel grateful for the chance to experience such a serene and stunning place. Walking through Gorky Park at night was an unforgettable experience that I would cherish forever.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: Что такое Работа с библиографией?
item.description:
Что такое Работа с библиографией? :

Работа с библиографией - это важный аспект любого исследовательского проекта или научной работы. Библиография представляет собой список использованных в работе источников, таких как книги, статьи, журналы, и т.д. Это необходимо для подтверждения авторства и удостоверения того, что автор основывает свои выводы на достоверной информации.

Какие способы работы с библиографией ?

Есть несколько способов работы с библиографией. Один из самых популярных - использование специализированных программ, таких как EndNote, Zotero, Mendeley, и других. Эти программы помогают создавать библиографические списки, управлять их и экспортировать в нужном формате.

Другой способ - это ручное создание библиографии. Для этого нужно внимательно отслеживать использованные источники и форматировать их соответствующим образом. Существует несколько стилей оформления библиографических списков, таких как APA, MLA, Chicago, и т.д. Важно выбрать нужный стиль и следовать его правилам.

Независимо от выбранного метода, работа с библиографией - это процесс, требующий внимания и тщательности. Важно не только правильно оформить библиографию, но и убедиться в ее полноте и точности. Это поможет автору не только подтвердить свою работу, но и облегчит процесс дальнейшей работы, так как использованные источники будут удобно организованы и доступны.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: A journey on how i passed the start of my week!
item.description:
Monday was such a journey 🔥 :

As a first-year computer science student from Algeria studying in Moscow, I was excited about the week ahead. I had prepared a presentation on DNS architecture and was looking forward to presenting it to my classmates online. I had spent many hours researching and preparing for the presentation, and I was eager to share my knowledge and insights with my peers.

On Monday, I woke up early and joined my online class. I delivered my presentation . I was glad that my hard work had paid off and that I had been able to contribute to the class discussion.

After the presentation, I went to the university to attend my Russian language class. I had been studying Russian for more than 10 months and was making good progress in learning the language. It was challenging, but I was determined to become proficient in Russian, as it would be useful for my future career as a computer scientist.

In the evening, I decided to explore a neighborhood called Prospekt Vernadskogo. I had heard that it was a vibrant and bustling area, with many shops, restaurants, and cafes. I walked around the streets, admiring the colorful buildings and the lively atmosphere. I stopped at a cafe and ordered some tea and pastries, enjoying the cozy ambiance and the friendly staff.

As the day came to an end, I felt satisfied with what I had accomplished and experienced. I had delivered a successful presentation, studied a new language for more than 10 months, and explored a new neighborhood. I knew that there would be more challenges and opportunities in the weeks and months ahead, but I felt ready and excited to face them.

After my eventful day of presenting, studying, and exploring, I was exhausted. I walked back to my dormitory, feeling the fatigue settling in. I couldn’t wait to lie down and rest after the long day.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: Iftar (dinner) in the Algerian embassy in Moscow
item.description:
On Friday, I had the opportunity to visit our Algerian embassy in Moscow to break my fast (iftar) during the holy month of Ramadan. I was delighted to see that the majority of the attendees were students, which created a lively and warm atmosphere. The food was nothing short of excellent, with a wide variety of traditional Algerian dishes that were well-prepared and flavorful. I must say that the organizers did an exceptional job of ensuring that everything ran smoothly, from the service to the seating arrangements. Overall, it was a wonderful experience that truly reflected the spirit of Ramadan and Algerian hospitality.

As I sat down to enjoy my meal, I couldn’t help but appreciate the effort that went into creating such a warm and inviting ambiance. The room was beautifully decorated with traditional Algerian ornaments, and the aroma of the delicious food filled the air. I had the chance to chat with some of the other attendees, and we shared stories about our experiences studying in Moscow. The sense of camaraderie and togetherness was palpable, and it made for a truly memorable evening. All in all, it was a great way to connect with my fellow Algerians, share some wonderful food, and celebrate the holy month of Ramadan.

  • As the iftar drew to a close, I noticed the organizers busy preparing take-away boxes filled with delicious food for suhoor. It was a thoughtful gesture that truly demonstrated their hospitality and generosity. Before leaving, I made sure to express my gratitude to the organizers for their hard work and dedication in creating such a wonderful event. As I stepped out of the embassy, I felt a sense of warmth and camaraderie, knowing that I had just shared a special moment with my fellow Algerians. It was an evening that I will always cherish, and I look forward to attending more such events in the future.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: What Is GitHub? A Beginner’s Introduction to GitHub
item.description:
At a high level, GitHub is a website and cloud-based service that helps developers store and manage their code, as well as track and control changes to their code. To understand exactly what GitHub is, you need to know two connected principles:

  • Version control
  • Git In this article, we’ll first explain those two principles. Then, we’ll dig into more about GitHub and how you can actually use GitHub to work with WordPress and/or Kinsta.

What Is Version Control?

Version control helps developers track and manage changes to a software project’s code. As a software project grows, version control becomes essential. Take WordPress…

At this point, WordPress is a pretty big project. If a core developer wanted to work on one specific part of the WordPress codebase, it wouldn’t be safe or efficient to have them directly edit the “official” source code.

Instead, version control lets developers safely work through branching and merging.

With branching, a developer duplicates part of the source code (called the repository). The developer can then safely make changes to that part of the code without affecting the rest of the project.

Then, once the developer gets his or her part of the code working properly, he or she can merge that code back into the main source code to make it official.

All of these changes are then tracked and can be reverted if need be.

What Is Git?

Git is a specific open-source version control system created by Linus Torvalds in 2005.

Specifically, Git is a distributed version control system, which means that the entire codebase and history is available on every developer’s computer, which allows for easy branching and merging.

According to a Stack Overflow developer survey, over 87% of developers use Git.

What Is GitHub?

GitHub is a for-profit company that offers a cloud-based Git repository hosting service. Essentially, it makes it a lot easier for individuals and teams to use Git for version control and collaboration.

GitHub’s interface is user-friendly enough so even novice coders can take advantage of Git. Without GitHub, using Git generally requires a bit more technical savvy and use of the command line.

GitHub is so user-friendly, though, that some people even use GitHub to manage other types of projects – like writing books

Additionally, anyone can sign up and host a public code repository for free, which makes GitHub especially popular with open-source projects.

As a company, GitHub makes money by selling hosted private code repositories, as well as other business-focused plans that make it easier for organizations to manage team members and security. We utilize Github extensively at Kinsta to manage and develop internal projects.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: What Is markdown? A Beginner’s Introduction to markdown
item.description:
What is Markdown?

Markdown is a lightweight markup language that describes how text should look on a page. HTML is another example of a markup language. Markdown is a style of writing documents that makes it easy to define what the content should look like. It describes headers, text styles, links, lists and so much more.

Markdown is used in documentation, articles, and notes and can even be used to build a webpage. If you use GitHub, you’ll be familiar with the “readme.md” files that show up in the root of a repository. That “md” stands for markdown. You can even create a readme on your GitHub profile to customize your GitHub profile page. Example of GitHub readme.md file:

  • It’s a very readable syntax and it can be converted into HTML, XHTML as well as in other formats. Its main purpose is readability and ease of use.
  • Markdown Editors: You can write markdown in any text editor, Markpad, HarooPad, MarkdownPad2 and Typora, but it’s really nice to have something that gives you a preview of what it is going to look like. So what we are going to use is Visual Studio Code(VS Code) with an extension called “Auto Open Markdown Previewer’’. Given below is the image of the extension that we are going to use:

Why Should You Use Markdown?

  • Simple Learning Curve: Markdown is very simple to learn. The official syntax can be found on the website: daringfireball. However, you need to know that typing word will make it bold, typing word will change that word to italics, and adding a - sign before the word will create lists. Also, it is much easier to read raw Markdown than raw HTML.
  • Easy HTML Conversion: Markdown has built-in software to convert a plain text to HTML. Hence it can also be considered as a text-to-HTML conversion software in addition to being a markup language.
  • Create Static Sites Easily: Markdown empowers you to make free, simple, and static sites utilizing open-source tools like Mkdocs, Jekyll, or Read the Docs.
  • Easy Sharing and Syncing: You can easily sync and share files created in the Markdown editor to Dropbox, Google Drive, and WordPress.
  • Diversification: Since Markdown is just plain text, it can be converted into a bunch of formats like PDF, epub, Docx, HTML, etc. To acquire in-depth skills in web development, visit KnowledgeHut’s online Web Development Course and learn development from scratch.

How Does Markdown Work?

Working of Markdown can be explained in the following four steps:

  1. Create Markdown file: The first step is to create a Markdown file with “.md” or “.markdown” extension.
  2. Open File in Markdown Application: You need a Markdown application capable of processing the Markdown file. There are lots of applications available such as Typora, Ghostwriter, etc.
  3. Working of Markdown Application: Markdown applications use Markdown processors to take the Markdown-formatted text and output it to HTML format.
  4. View the file in a web browser: The final rendered output of your document can now be viewed in a web browser.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.

item.title: A journey on how i passed last week
item.description:
Exploring a new sphere:

  • So! In this week i have decided to join training about business modeling and how to launch a project from scratch! it was in friday at 18:00 in the daculty of economy in which our trainer was waiting for us and he is from Germany.In the first session i got to know new people after that we created teams to work together on developing our ideas into real business projects.

💡 The main idea that i understood:

  • 🔀 A business model isn’t something direct but a chain of actions where order is so important.
  • 🔥 Having a reliable team is one the most important keys for a successful business model.

License

Copyright 2023-present Mougari Abderrahim.

Released under the MGR license.
